archive_data.question_text: In which of the following years was the Interim Government of India formed from the newly elected Constituent Assembly?

explanation.english: The Interim Government of India was formed on 2 September 1946 from the newly elected Constituent Assembly. This functioned until 15 August 1947 when India gained independence.
explanation.tanglish: India oda temporary government 1946 September 2nd Constituent Assembly la irundhu form pannapattathu. India swathanthram adaindhathukku munnadi idhu function pannithu.

archive_data.question_text: In which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution is the Finance Bill mentioned?

explanation.english: Article 110 of the Indian Constitution deals with Money Bills, which are a category of Finance Bills. While Article 117 also deals with Financial Bills, Article 110 is the primary provision for Money Bills that contain taxation provisions.
explanation.tanglish: Indian Constitution la Article 110 Money Bills pathi sollum. Idhu Finance Bills oda category. Article 117 um Financial Bills pathi sollum, ana Article 110 than tax sambanthiya bills ku mukkiyam.

archive_data.question_text: In India, how many sedimentary basins are there which cover an area of 3.14 million km2?

explanation.english: India has 26 sedimentary basins covering an area of approximately 3.14 million square kilometres. These basins are important for petroleum and natural gas exploration.
explanation.tanglish: India la 26 sedimentary basins irukku, adhu 3.14 million km2 area cover pannuthu. Ithu petrol matrum natural gas thedurathuku mukkiyamana edam.

archive_data.question_text: Jhijhiya is the famous cultural dance of which of the following states?

explanation.english: Jhijhiya is a traditional folk dance of Bihar, performed primarily during the Durga Puja festival. Women perform this dance while balancing lanterns on their heads.
explanation.tanglish: Jhijhiya idhu Bihar oda traditional folk dance. Durga Puja time la aaduvanga. Pengal than mathathala mela villaku vechundu aaduvanga.

archive_data.question_text: Nitrous oxide is a colourless and odourless substance that is also known as ______.

explanation.english: Nitrous oxide (N₂O) is commonly known as laughing gas because inhaling it produces euphoric effects and can cause giggling or laughter. It is used in medicine as an anesthetic.
explanation.tanglish: Nitrous oxide ah laughing gas nu soldranga. Ithu inhale pannum bothu sirippu matrum sandhosha feeling varum. Maruthuvathil anesthesia kaaga use pannuvanga.

archive_data.question_text: _______ is a branch of horticulture as it deals with the cultivation of flowers and ornamental crops from the time of planting to the time of harvesting.

explanation.english: Floriculture is the branch of horticulture concerned with the cultivation of flowering and ornamental plants. Sericulture is silk production, apiculture is beekeeping, and mariculture is marine farming.
explanation.tanglish: Floriculture nu flower maram ornamental plants valarpathu. Sericulture nu pattu paduthal, apiculture nu then valarthal, mariculture nu kadal valarthal.

archive_data.question_text: The CADC is formed under the 6th Schedule to the Constitution of India in 1972, for the Chakma ethnic people. What does CADC stand for?

explanation.english: CADC stands for Chakma Autonomous District Council. It was formed in 1972 under the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution in Mizoram to provide autonomy to the Chakma tribal people.
explanation.tanglish: CADC nu Chakma Autonomous District Council nu artham. Constitution oda 6th Schedule ku keel 1972 la Mizoram la Chakma makkaluku thanithuvam korikka form pannapattathu.

archive_data.question_text: How many committees were set up by the Constituent Assembly for framing the Constitution?

explanation.english: The Constituent Assembly appointed 13 committees (including major committees like the Drafting Committee, Union Constitution Committee, and Provincial Constitution Committee) to draft different parts of the Constitution.
explanation.tanglish: Constituent Assembly 13 committees appoint pannathu Constitution frame panna. Drafting Committee, Union Constitution Committee ithalam athula mukkiyamana avai.

archive_data.question_text: The Dhyan Chand Award, named after the legendary player Major Dhyan Chand, is awarded for the lifetime contribution in the sports field. In which of the following sports did he represent India?

explanation.english: Major Dhyan Chand was a legendary Indian field hockey player who represented India in hockey. He is considered one of the greatest hockey players of all time and won three Olympic gold medals.
explanation.tanglish: Major Dhyan Chand legendariyaana Indian hockey player. India ku hockey la represent pannar. Athigamana hockey vilayadargalil onnu. Moonru Olympic gold medal vangirukkar.

archive_data.question_text: ______ deal(s) with the taxation and expenditure decisions of the Government.

explanation.english: Fiscal Policy refers to the government's use of taxation and spending to influence the economy. Monetary policy is managed by the central bank (RBI) and deals with interest rates and money supply.
explanation.tanglish: Fiscal Policy nu government oda tax matrum selava decisions edukurathu. Monetary policy nu Reserve Bank of India manage pannum, interest rate matrum panchu supply pathi.

archive_data.question_text: Who among the following women army officers has been promoted to the second-highest rank of Lieutenant General in the Indian Army in March 2020?

explanation.english: Lt. Gen. Madhuri Kanitkar was promoted to the rank of Lieutenant General in March 2020, becoming the third woman in the Indian Army to hold this rank. She was appointed as Deputy Chief of Integrated Defence Staff.
explanation.tanglish: Lt. Gen. Madhuri Kanitkar March 2020 la Lieutenant General rank ku promote aanar. Indian Army la ivalo rank adaindha moondra pen. Integrated Defence Staff ku Deputy Chief ah niruthapattar.
